data.title: Monarch Surveyors IPO closes with 231 times subscription: Check GMP, listing & allotment dates
data.description:
Monarch Surveyors IPO

Pune-based Monarch Surveyors and Engineering Consultants closed its initial public offering on July 24 with a massive 231.43 times subscription on overwhelming response from across categories of investors.

The company that provides topographic surveys, project management consultancy to GIS mapping, and feasibility studies is raising Rs 93.75 crore via public issue of 37.5 lakh shares at Rs 250 per share.

Investors applied for 62.22 crore equity shares during July 22-24, compared to the offer size of 26.88 lakh shares via 3.18 lakh applications.

Retail and non-institutional investors took the lead, subscribing 263.01 times, and 228.62 times their allotted quota. The portion set aside for qualified institutional buyers was booked 179.01 times.

The company will finalise the IPO share allotment by July 25, and its equity shares will be available for trading on the BSE SME effective July 29.

The grey market premium for Monarch Surveyors IPO shares surged further to 70-80 percent, from 65 percent in previous session, the market observers said. The grey market is unofficial market for trading in IPO shares till the listing.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

The company that provides comprehensive consultancy services under concept to commissioning of infrastructure projects (including survey, design and technical supervision for roads, railways, metros, town planning, geospatial, mapping, land acquisition, water, transmission line, pipelines and other civil engineering sectors) intends to utilise Rs 32 crore of IPO proceed for purchase of machinery, Rs 30 crore for working capital requirement, and the remainder funds for general corporate purposes.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Beeline Capital Advisors acted as the merchant banker for the Monarch Surveyors IPO.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: NSDL IPO to open next week for subscription; IPO dates, size among 10 key things to know
data.description:
NSDL IPO to open next week for public subscription.

The much-awaited National Securities Depository Ltd (NSDL) is set to launch its initial public offering (IPO) next week in the primary market. As of December 31, 2024, NSDL is the largest depository in India.

Here the key things you need to know before the issue opens for public subscription:

1) NSDL IPO Dates: NSDL IPO will open for public subscription next week on July 30 and will conclude on August 1.

2) NSDL IPO Size: The issue size is pegged at around Rs 4,000 crore.

3) NSDL IPO Bidding for Anchor Investors: The one-day bidding for anchor investors is scheduled to open on July 29 ahead of the depository's maiden public issue.

4) NSDL IPO Details: The IPO only consists of Offer For Sale (OFS) component of 5.01 crore shares. Since the public issue is entirely an OFS, NSDL will not receive any proceeds from the IPO.

5) NSDL IPO: Who all are selling shares?

Those selling shares under this are -- the National Stock Exchange of India (NSE), State Bank of India (SBI), HDFC Bank, IDBI Bank, Union Bank of India and Administrator of Specified Undertaking of the Unit Trust of India (SUUTI).

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

6) NSDL IPO Listing Date: Shares of the company are expected to list on August 6 (Wednesday).

7) NSDL Peers in markets: This upcoming listing will make NSDL the country's second publicly traded depository after Central Depository Services (CDSL), which was listed on the NSE in 2017.

8) Largest Depository in India: As of December 31, 2024, NSDL is the largest depository in India in terms of number of issuers, number of active instruments, market share in demat value of settlement volume and value of assets held under custody.

9) Key financials: For the full financial year 2024-25, the depository's net profit surged by 24.57 per cent to Rs 343 crore and total income rose to Rs 1,535 crore, a 12.41 per cent increase over FY 2023-24.

10) About the company: It is a Sebi-registered market infrastructure institution offering a wide range of products and services to the financial and securities markets in India. Following the introduction of the Depositories Act in 1996, NSDL pioneered the dematerialisation of securities in India in November 1996.

IDBI Bank & NSE-backed NSDL plans $463-mn IPO launch next week at $1.85-bn valuation

On December 5, 2022, Moneycontrol was the first to report that NSDL had kicked off its IPO plans, a pure OFS (offer for sale) with NSE and IDBI Bank planning to sell stake.

The introduction of the depository system brought about a notable transformation in trade settlement practices on stock exchanges and played a pivotal role in the implementation of rolling settlements in India, the addendum added.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Shanti Gold International IPO to open on July 25; anchor book closes with bids worth Rs 108 crore
data.description:
Shanti Gold on Thursday has finalised allocation of 54.29 lakh shares to 15 anchor investors at Rs 199 per equity share.

Mumbai-based gold jewellery maker Shanti Gold International has mobilised Rs 108.03 crore via anchor book on July 24, a day before its IPO opening.

Its initial public offering will open for subscription on July 25 and close on July 29, with price band of Rs 189-199 per share.

Shanti Gold on Thursday has finalised allocation of 54.29 lakh shares to 15 anchor investors at Rs 199 per equity share.

Societe Generale, Wealthwave Capital Fund, Vijit Growth Fund, Founders Collective Fund, Smart Horizon Opportunity Fund, Swyom India Alpha Fund, and Sunrise Investment Opportunities Fund were among 15 investors participated in the anchor book.

Incorporated in 2003, Shanti Gold aims to Rs 360.11 crore via entirely fresh issue of 1.8 crore equity shares at the upper price band.

The 22kt CZ casting gold jewellery maker will spend Rs 46.3 crore of IPO proceeds for setting up of the proposed Jaipur facility, and Rs 200 crore for working capital requirements. Further, Rs 17 crore will be used for repayment of debt, and the remainder funds for general corporate purposes.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

Currently, with manufacturing facility in Mumbai having capacity of 2,700 kg per annum, the company makes jewellery products for several corporate jewellery brands such as Joyalukkas India, Lalithaa Jewellery Mart, Alukkas Enterprises, Vysyaraju Jewellers, and Shree Kalptaru Jewellers.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

After Jaipur expansion, its manufacturing capacity will increase by 1,200 kgs, to a total of 3,900 kgs.

Choice Capital Advisors is acting as the book running lead manager for the Shanti Gold International IPO.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: M&B Engineering to launch Rs 650-crore IPO on July 30
data.description:
M&B Engineering IPO

Gujarat-based turnkey PEB and roofing solutions provider M&B Engineering has filed Red Herring Prospectus (RHP) with the Registrar of Companies on July 24 to launch its Rs 650-crore initial share sale for public subscription on July 30. Its draft papers were approved by the SEBI on June 11 this year.

The IPO consists of fresh issuance of equity shares worth Rs 275 crore, and an offer-for-sale of Rs 375 crore worth shares by promoters.

This would be the 13th initial public offering (IPO) in the mainboard segment hitting Dalal Street in current month, and would be the third public issue opening on July 30 along with NSDL, and Sri Lotus Developers.

The anchor book of the IPO will be launched for institutional investors on July 29. The offer will close for all kind of investors on August 1.

M&B Engineering will finalise IPO share allotment by August 4, followed by the listing of shares on the bourses scheduled on August 6.

The company has reserved up to 75 percent of its IPO size for qualified institutional buyers. Further, 15 percent shares are set aside for non-institutional investors, and the remainder 10 percent for retail investors.

M&B Engineering provides pre-engineered buildings (PEBs) and self-supported steel roofing solutions to several sectors including general engineering and manufacturing, food and beverages, warehousing and logistics, power, textiles, and railways. It has executed over 9,500 projects till March 2025 under its Phenix and Proflex divisions.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

It has installed capacity of 1,03,800 MTPA in PEB structures and 18 lakh square metres per annum for self-supported roofing solutions.

The company that competes with listed peers like Pennar Industries, Bansal Roofing Products, BirlaNU, Everest Industries, and Interarch Building Products will spend Rs 130.6 crore out of fresh issue proceeds for purchase of equipment and machinery, building works, solar rooftop grid and transport vehicles.

Further, Rs 5.2 crore will be invested in information technology software upgradation, and Rs 58.8 crore will be used for repaying debt. As of May 2025, its outstanding borrowings were Rs 186.1 crore.

On the financial front, M&B Engineering has recorded profit at Rs 77 crore for the year ended March 2025, growing 68.8 percent over the previous year, and in the same period, revenue soared 24.3 percent to Rs 988.6 crore.

Equirus Capital, and DAM Capital Advisors are merchant bankers managing the M&B Engineering IPO.

data.title: FIIs net sell shares worth Rs 2,134 crore, DIIs net buy Rs 2,617 crore on July 24
data.description:
For the year so far, FIIs have been net sellers of shares worth Rs 1.44 lakh crore, while DIIs have net bought Rs 3.85 lakh crore worth of shares.


Foreign institutional investors (FII/FPI) were net sellers on July 24, selling shares worth Rs 2,134 crore while domestic institutional investors (DII) were net buyers of shares worth Rs 2,617 crore, provisional data showed.

During the trading session, DIIs net bought shares worth Rs 13,507 crore and sold shares worth Rs 10,890 crore. FIIs purchased shares worth Rs 13,725 crore and sold shares worth Rs 15,859 crore.

For the year so far, FIIs have been net sellers of shares worth Rs 1.44 lakh crore, while DIIs have net bought Rs 3.85 lakh crore worth of shares.


Market view

At close, the Sensex was down 542.47 points or 0.66 percent at 82,184.17, and the Nifty was down 157.80 points or 0.63 percent at 25,062.10. BSE Midcap and smallcap indices down 0.4 percent each. Eternal, Dr Reddy's Labs, Tata Motors, Grasim Industries, Cipla were among major gainers on the Nifty, while losers included Trent, Nestle India, Shriram Finance, Tech Mahindra, Reliance Industries.

On the sectoral front, except PSU Bank (up 1.2 percnet) and pharma (up 0.5 percent), all the other indices ended in the red.

Vinod Nair, Head of Research, Geojit Investments Limited noted that Indian equities fell sharply, reversing previous gains despite positive global cues. "Initial optimism around the India-UK free trade agreement gave way to caution as attention shifted firmly to earnings. The IT and FMCG sectors dragged down large-cap stocks due to subdued Q1 performance. Though the Q1 earnings are broadly in line, it does not justify the premium valuation; India is trading at a 3-year high of 21x P/E," he said.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Nasdaq opens higher on Alphabet earnings; Dow dragged by IBM and Tesla slump
data.description:
The S&P 500 edged up 0.2%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 302 points, or 0.7%, pressured by a 6% fall in IBM

US stocks opened on a mixed note Thursday as upbeat earnings from Alphabet lifted tech shares, while IBM and Tesla weighed on the Dow. The Nasdaq Composite rose 0.4% in early trade, powered by a 3% gain in Alphabet after the tech giant posted better-than-expected Q2 earnings and revenue.

The S&P 500 edged up 0.2%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 302 points, or 0.7%, pressured by a 6% fall in IBM after the company missed software revenue estimates. Tesla also dropped 6% as automotive revenue declined for a second straight quarter.

Investors are also tracking fresh political tension, with US President Donald Trump set to visit the Federal Reserve later today — a highly unusual move that underscores his growing public criticism of Chair Jerome Powell. This would mark the first official visit by a sitting US president to the Fed in nearly 20 years.

The mixed open comes after Wednesday’s strong rally, which saw the S&P 500 notch its 12th record close of the year and the Nasdaq Composite cross 21,000 for the first time. Gains were supported by easing global trade tensions. A Financial Times report said the US and EU are nearing a tariff deal that could raise levies to 15% on EU imports. Bloomberg cited diplomatic sources confirming the progress.

“The market is being driven by a cocktail of strong tech earnings, receding trade uncertainty and now some real political theatre,” said Jeremy Siegel, professor at Wharton and senior economist at WisdomTree. “There’s enough momentum here to keep stocks buoyant, but the Fed visit throws in a wildcard.”

Global markets displayed cautious optimism as investors assessed mixed signals from key economies. In Asia, Japan’s Nikkei 225 gained 0.8%, buoyed by a weaker yen and positive export data, while China’s CSI 300 slipped 0.3% amid ongoing concerns over property sector debt and regulatory pressures.

In Europe, the Stoxx 600 rose 0.5%, supported by progress in US-EU tariff talks, though uncertainty lingers over potential disruptions from a looming U.K. general election. Crude oil prices steadied, with Brent crude at $79 per barrel, as OPEC+ signaled no immediate changes to production cuts despite rising Middle East tensions.

Meanwhile, a surprise uptick in Germany’s Ifo Business Climate Index to 87.2 for July bolstered hopes of a eurozone recovery, though persistent inflation concerns kept markets on edge. These global dynamics, combined with anticipation of the Federal Reserve’s next moves, are shaping a complex backdrop for US equities.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Fujiyama Power, Saatvik Green, Pranav Constructions among six IPOs to get Sebi nod
data.description:
IPO News

Capital market regulator Sebi has approved six IPOs including Vinir Engineering, Pranav Constructions, and Fujiyama Power Systems, while Saatvik Green Energy, SIS Cash Services and Anlon Healthcare are among others that have received approval.

Apart from these, the pre-filing papers of edtech major Physicswallah too has been cleared by the regulator on July 18.

Sebi has issued observations on the draft papers of Fujiyama Power Systems, and Saatvik Green Energy on July 14 and July 15, respectively, while the Vinir Engineering, Pranav Constructions, and SIS Cash Services' preliminary papers have received observations from the regulator on July 17, followed by Anlon Healthcare on July 18, as per the processing status of draft offer documents published on July 24.

Uttar Pradesh-based rooftop solar solutions provider Fujiyama Power Systems had filed draft papers with the Sebi on December 28, 2024 to raise funds via IPO which will be a combination of fresh issuance of shares worth Rs 700 crore, and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of 2 crore shares by promoters.

Solar photovoltaic modules manufacturer Saatvik Green Energy, which had filed preliminary papers on November 18 last year, plans to mobilise Rs 1,150 crore via initial share sale. The Gurugram-based company intends to raise Rs 850 crore via fresh issue, and Rs 300 crore via OFS.

Bangalore-based Vinir Engineering, which makes precision-forged and machined components for several industries (like energy, defence, aerospace, railways, energy turbines, hydraulics, earthmoving, and high-end engineering), filed draft red herring prospectus with the Sebi on January 17 this year. It has planned for fund raising entirely via offer-for-sale of 5.33 crore shares with no fresh issue component.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

Mumbai-based real estate developer Pranav Constructions is looking to tap markets to fund its redevelopment expenses. It filed draft papers on February 28 this year for an IPO which will be a mix of fresh issuance of shares worth Rs 392 crore, and an offer-for-sale of 28.56 lakh shares by promoter and investor.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

New Delhi-based cash logistics solutions provider SIS Cash Services filed DRHP with the regulator on March 27 this year for IPO, which will comprise of fresh issue of Rs 100 crore and an OFS of 37.15 lakh shares by promoters.

Anlon Healthcare approached capital markets to raise funds for its expansion of manufacturing facility, repaying of debt, and working capital requirements. The Gujarat-based chemical manufacturing company filed draft papers on October 9 last year for IPO which will consist of solely fresh issue of 1.4 crore shares.

News reports had suggested that edtech firm Physicswallah, which filed draft papers through the confidential route, is looking to raise around Rs 4,600 crore via public issue which consists of both fresh issue and offer-for-sale.

Gaudium IVF and Women Health has withdrawn its preliminary papers on January 29. The New Delhi-based in-vitro fertilization treatment provider had filed draft papers in January this year for IPO which was comprised of fresh issue of 1.83 crore equity shares, and an offer-for-sale of 25.31 lakh shares by the promoter.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Brigade Hotel, GNG Electronics, Indiqube Spaces IPO GMPs surge up to 45% amid strong investor interest - check details
data.description:
Brigade Hotel, GNG Electronics, Indiqube Spaces IPO GMPs surge up to 45% amid strong investor interest.

The initial public offerings (IPOs) of Brigade Hotel Ventures, GNG Electronics and IndiQube Spaces witnessed strong investor interest in the primary market, with grey market premiums (GMPs) rising as much as 45 percent, according to Investorgain.

IPOGMP (Rs)GMP (%)
GNG Electronics10042.19
IndiQube Spaces125
Brigade Hotel Ventures6.57.22

Brigade Hotel Ventures Ltd's IPO opened for subscription on Thursday and was subscribed 63 percent on the first day. The public issue will remain open till July 28. The price band has been fixed at Rs 85–90 per share.

Meanwhile, IndiQube Spaces’ Rs 700-crore IPO was fully subscribed on the second day of bidding. The offer received 2.54 times subscription, as per data available on the NSE website.

GNG Electronics Ltd’s IPO, which opened earlier this week, continued to see robust demand. On the second day of subscription, the offer received bids for 38.16 crore shares against 1.41 crore shares on offer.

The category for Non-Institutional Investors was subscribed 68.57 times, while the Retail Individual Investors (RIIs) portion saw 23.01 times subscription. The Qualified Institutional Buyers (QIBs) segment was subscribed 2.21 times. The company had raised Rs 138 crore from anchor investors on Tuesday.

The Rs 460-crore IPO will close on July 25. The price band is set at Rs 225–237 per share, valuing the company at over Rs 2,700 crore at the upper end.

data.title: Wall Street futures mixed as investors weigh Tesla, Alphabet results; Trump's visit to Fed HQ eyed
data.description:
Wall Street futures mixed as investors weigh Tesla, Alphabet results; Trump's visit to Fed HQ eyed

US equity futures were mixed Thursday morning as investors digested sharply divergent earnings from Big Tech, tracked renewed trade optimism with Japan and the EU, and braced for a potentially historic visit by President Donald Trump to the Federal Reserve headquarters later today.

At 7:19 a.m. ET, S&P 500 futures were up 0.1 percent, while Nasdaq 100 futures gained 0.3 percent, buoyed by Alphabet’s strong quarterly print. Dow futures, however, fell 198 points or 0.4 percent, dragged lower by disappointing earnings from IBM and Dow Inc.

Tech earnings presented a split picture overnight. Alphabet shares rose nearly 4 percent in premarket trading after the Google parent beat expectations on both revenue and profit, while also raising its capital spending guidance. Tesla, in contrast, dropped around 6 percent as auto revenue declined for a second consecutive quarter. The EV maker has been underperforming its Big Tech peers through 2025, and investors appeared unwilling to give it further benefit of doubt.

IBM slid nearly 6 percent after missing software revenue estimates, and Dow Inc. plunged more than 7 percent after posting a wider-than-expected quarterly loss and missing topline forecasts. The chemical major reported a loss of 42 cents per share, well below the Street's expectation of a 17-cent loss.

Adding to market uncertainty, the White House confirmed that President Trump will visit the Federal Reserve at 4 p.m. ET — the first such visit by a sitting US president in nearly two decades. The meeting comes just ahead of next week’s policy review, with traders already pricing in a 97 percent chance the Fed will hold rates steady, according to the CME FedWatch tool. However, Trump has repeatedly criticized Fed Chair Jerome Powell for not cutting rates faster, potentially straining the central bank’s perceived independence.

Despite these headwinds, investor sentiment remains cautiously constructive, following Wednesday’s rally across major indices. The S&P 500 rose 0.78 percent to notch its 12th record close of 2025, the Dow gained over 507 points, just shy of a new all-time high, and the Nasdaq Composite breached the 21,000 mark for the first time.

Markets also took cues from Trump’s declaration of a “massive” trade agreement with Japan, which he claimed includes 15 percent reciprocal tariffs and a promised $550-billion investment into the US. However, images shared by the White House showed apparent last-minute edits to the deal’s terms, casting some doubt on its immediate scope. Reports also suggest progress toward a similar EU trade pact, which could include coordinated levies on imports.

Meanwhile, a surge in meme stocks like GoPro and Krispy Kreme signaled risk-on behavior among retail traders. Both names saw double-digit intraday moves on Wednesday, driven more by social media buzz and short interest than by fundamentals — echoing the speculative energy of the 2021 retail trading wave.

Thursday’s earnings docket remains packed, with Honeywell, Nasdaq, Blackstone, Union Pacific, and American Airlines reporting before the bell. Intel, Newmont and Boyd Gaming are among the names set to report after market close.

Asian Market

Markets across Asia closed mostly higher on Thursday, buoyed by optimism surrounding fresh US-Japan and US–EU trade developments. In Tokyo, the Nikkei 225 surged 1.6 percent to 41,826, while the Topix hit a record high of 2,977.55. Hong Kong’s Hang Seng rose 0.5 percent to 25,667, and China’s CSI 300 added 0.7 percent, supported by improved global sentiment

Indian shares closed lower on Thursday amid persistent selling in IT and consumer stocks, and concerns about global uncertainty — particularly around U.S.–India trade talks and President Trump’s surprise Fed visit.

The Sensex fell 0.66 percent to 82,184, while the Nifty 50 dropped 0.63 percent to 25,062 The Times of India. The IT-heavy Nifty IT index plunged about 2.2 percent, weighed down by a decline in earnings sentiment among technology majors. Consumer stocks also lagged, with Nestlé India sliding over 5 percent on margin concerns Reuters.

The benchmark indices underperformed regional peers, extending July’s underwhelming run — down roughly 1.8 percent compared to a 4 percent rally in other Asia-Pacific markets

Australia (ASX 200)

The ASX 200 closed down approximately 0.3%, finishing at 8,709–8,709.4 points. The drop came amid broad sector weakness, led by industrials, energy, consumer discretionary, and property, despite healthcare and select bank stocks faring better. Reserve Bank Governor Michele Bullock’s remarks—including a lack of urgency on rate cuts—cooled rate-cut expectations, dragging sentiment
data.author: MoneyControl
